rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Surcharge, Attorney'S Fees And Costs (These Are The Remaining Issues From Jerome Black'S Petition Filed On 7/12/18)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no response to the examiner notes. a. In response to the Examiner notes a Declaration was filed by the attorney. Former co-trustees must sign and verify pleadings. [CRC 7.103]. b. Please have the representative amend the petition to request compensation for this time period and give proper notice or have the petitioners waive compensation for this time period. c. Please explain on disbursements entry 10/24/2016 $25.00 to Pilgrim Baptist Church d. Please explain on disbursements entry 12/7/2016 $6.00 for Misc. e. Please explain how the court can approve an account with 3 unexplained withdrawals totaling $4,358.00 and an unexplained payment to Chase Collections for $306.19 2) Court to determine how best to proceed.

rulings.Calendar Matter: FILING OF FIRST ACCOUNT
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Limited conservators have not filed 1st account. No supplemental inventory and appraisal listing preliminary estate distribution to limited conservatee has been filed. Limited conservators remain without powers to possess and manage any property of limited conservatee. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/09/2021. Issues: 1) Please explain why $1,700,000.00 bond is sufficient when the I&A alleges $1,898,062.86 in assets. LRSF 14.16 Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond. See CRC 7.204. When the bond of a fiduciary must be increased, the Court favors filing of an additional bond rather than a substitute bond.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLENE D. LOWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/2/21. The "supplement" filed on 1/7/21 actually amended the petition to increase the attorney fees. [CRC 7.3.] As noted in the previous tentative ruling, an amendment would be required in order to correct the attorney fee request. An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] However, the statutory fee was correctly shown in the original petition as $31,320.00, 10% of which would be $3,132.00, making the reduced amount $28,188.00. If the attorney had accepted the amount set forth in the petition ($28,098.00), the petition, as supplemented, could have been pre-granted. Notice with a copy of the "supplement" that amended the petition must be given to all heirs at least 15 days before 2/2/21. (Letters were issued on 11/18/19.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA M. BUTCHART-FIORILLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/10/2021. Issues: 1) Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the Amendment. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7 C.] 2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. (CRC 7.53).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES L. WEYRO TESTAMENTARY TRUST DATED MARCH 31, 1976
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee And For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests an order directing the remainder of trust assets to Andrew Babin and Jeffrey Babin, as tenants in common. Attorney avers that, because this trust has not terminated, the trustee should be directed to distribute trust assets to Richard Babin's issue, pursuant to Settlor's intent. However, it appears that Settlor's intent was for Richard Babin to receive distribution upon Thelma Babin's death. Attorney also provides authority to find that Richard Babin's estate is the rightful beneficiary and requests that this Court provide clear instructions as to how property is to be distributed; however, the petition has not been properly amended to request this. 2) Petitioner requests appointment as successor trustee. He alleges that he is a beneficiary of the trust. 3) Petitioner requests waiver of bond as he and his brother, who are the only issue of Richard Babin, have waived bond. He alleges that the value of the only asset in the trust (an undeveloped real property), according to Placer Title Company's title guarantee, is $1,285, but the property has not been formally appraised by any other party. 4) This trust is referred to as the Myrtle Weyro Testamentary Trust and this case is titled as such; however, Charles Weyro's will established this trust for the benefit of Myrtle Weyro. Therefore, it appears the case should be titled as the "Charles L. Weyro Testamentary Trust fbo Myrtle Weyro". Attorney states that the petition was titled the way it is because the title to the sole asset of this trust, an undeveloped real property, is in "Myrtle V. Weyro, in trust", and petitioner wished to maintain consistency in the title. 5) Petitioner requests a finding that the Myrtle Weyro Testamentary Trust dated 3/31/76 is a valid and existing trust. A subsequent declaration has acknowledged that this finding is not necessary. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES HAROLD ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/21. Notes emailed 1/13/21. Among other issues... When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] When the whereabouts are unknown of a person entitled to notice, a declaration re due diligence must be filed. [CRC 7.52(a)] If, after diligent search, the person cannot be located, an ex parte petition must be filed to request the Court to dispense with notice to such person(s). Attachment 8 to the petition also does not set forth the relationship to the decedent or address for Linda Adams and Helen Adams. They are also missing from the service list. Notice was given to Rosanna Adams, shown as the decedent's sister-in-law, but not to Rosie Adams, who is a niece. The copy of the will does not name anyone in Article Three. How did the petitioner ascertain who was named as executor? The will also refers to a Personal Property Memorandum re tangible personal property. Was none found?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JUSTIN WINFREY REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Designating That Certain Real Property And Personal Property Belongs To The Justin Winfrey Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the sole purpose of determining if this is the proper venue. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. 1. The proper form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115, not DE-120. 2. Notice must be personally served on any person, other than a trustee or beneficiary, whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition. [Probate Code Section 850(a) and 17203(b) and CCP Section 413.10.] 3. A supplement to an 850 petition also must also be served (via mail to the beneficiaries of a trust and personally to anyone whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition). [CRC 7.53(b).] 4. As the sole heir-at-law is a minor, it appears that a guardian at litem should be appointed for the minor.
